article_title: Obligations of providers of high-risk AI systems
obligation_text: Providers of high-risk AI systems shall: ensure that their high-risk AI systems are compliant with the requirements set out in Section 2; have a quality management system in place which complies with Article 17; keep the documentation referred to in Article 18.
scoring_text: Knowledge of high-risk AI system compliance management, including regulatory requirement tracking, quality management system implementation, and documentation management for AI system providers.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of high-risk AI system compliance management, including regulatory requirement tracking, quality management system implementation, and documentation management for AI system providers.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of AI regulatory compliance requirements, including EU AI Act conformity assessment obligations, prohibited use categories, and documentation requirements for high-risk AI systems.
comments: EU AI Act Art. 16(a-c) (obligation) equal WIDAI AG domain. AG-K-008 is the strongest match (STS: 0.736, cosine: 0.809). Secondary: AG-T-011 (AG, cosine: 0.756), AG-K-013 (AG, cosine: 0.754).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the regulatory requirement) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 7.4/10 (raw 0.736). Shared vocabulary: [ai, compliance, documentation, high, knowledge, regulatory, risk]. EU AI Act competency implication terms [implementation, management, providers, quality, requirement, system] vs WIDAI terms [act, assessment, categories, conformity, eu, obligations] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.923 (P=0.922, R=0.924). Balanced P/R — the 22-word competency implication and 23-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs (mean ~0.007) because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (100.0%). Standard result for cross-framework competency mapping — the EU AI Act element and WIDAI KSA describe overlapping competencies without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.809 > STS 0.736 (gap +0.073). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [ai, compliance, documentation, high, knowledge] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 39.3%. Shared tokens: [ai, compliance, documentation, high, knowledge, regulatory, risk]. EU AI Act-only: [implementation, management, providers, quality, requirement, system]. WIDAI-only: [act, assessment, categories, conformity, eu, obligations].
